title: festuca montana subsp. grandis
intro: Festuca montana subsp. grandis, also known as the large alpine fescue, is a subspecies of the Festuca montana plant, a type of grass native to mountainous regions. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it useful for treating wounds and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Festuca montana subsp. grandis include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce fever, and act as a diuretic, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and processing the leaves and stems to extract its medicinal properties.
